message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="marty-cagan" color="blue" summary="Details/summary delete pattern looks good">
That's a smart pattern for US-7's delete confirmation -- `details/summary` gives us the two-click safety net without JS and without a modal. Lightweight, accessible, and progressive. Looking forward to seeing the proposal when Task 9 comes up.

One thing to keep in mind for that pattern: the accessible label on the delete button needs to include the todo title (e.g., "Delete: Buy groceries") per US-7 acceptance criteria. Make sure the summary element carries that context.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good point about the accessible label. Let me think about how the `details/summary` pattern would work with that and prepare CSS for it proactively, since Task 9 is active now.

content.text:
The delete button is already in the template (lines 49-54) with the accessible label `aria-label="Delete "{{ todo.title }}""`. It's currently a direct form submission with no confirmation step. US-7 says "Confirmation mechanism before deletion -- keep it lightweight, not a modal dialog."

The `details/summary` approach would replace the current direct delete button with an expandable confirmation. But this is a template change that needs team discussion -- the current implementation may be intentional as a simpler first pass. Let me respond to Marty and note the current state.

input.content:
Good point on the accessible label. The current template already includes it: `aria-label="Delete "{{ todo.title }}""` on the delete button (line 51 of todos.html). However, the current implementation is a direct form submission with no confirmation step. US-7 requires "Confirmation mechanism before deletion -- keep it lightweight, not a modal dialog." If the team wants the `details/summary` pattern, the template would look like: ```html
✕
Delete this todo?
``` The `` click opens the confirmation, the form inside submits the actual delete. Closing the `
` (click elsewhere or Escape) cancels. No JS needed. I'll flag this for the Rust teammates working on Task 9.
input.summary: Delete label done, confirmation pattern needs team discussion

content.text: Let me flag the missing confirmation to the team-lead since it's an acceptance criteria gap.

input.content:
Heads up on Task 9: the delete button is already in the template (line 49-54 of todos.html) with proper `aria-label`, but it's a direct form submission with **no confirmation step**. US-7 requires "Confirmation mechanism before deletion -- keep it lightweight, not a modal dialog."

I've proposed a `details/summary` pattern to Marty that provides two-click delete safety with pure HTML (no JS needed). The Rust teammates working on Task 9 should be aware this needs to be added before the task can be considered complete per the acceptance criteria.
input.summary: Task 9 missing required delete confirmation per US-7

content.text: Now let me add CSS for the `details/summary` confirmation pattern so it's ready when the template is updated.

content.content: The file /home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/css/main.css has been updated successfully.

lines: -  /* Delete button -- small, muted, danger on hover. Per US-7: button not link. */
+   * Summary shows the delete icon; opening reveals confirm form. */
+  /* Delete trigger -- the summary element styled as a small muted button */
+  /* Remove default summary marker in WebKit/Blink */
+  /* Confirmation controls revealed when <details> is open */

input.command:
git add /home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/css/main.css && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add delete confirmation CSS using details/summary pattern

Styles for the two-click delete flow per US-7: summary element
as delete trigger, details[open] reveals confirm form with
danger-colored "Yes" button. No JavaScript required.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push
